title: Ports, Immutable Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds explicitly owned crawler component graph from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of explicitly owned crawler component graph while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Create readonly branded identities only after unknown runtime values pass current validation and normalization.
Assign each Promise a caller, settlement path, concurrency slot, cancellation rule, error destination, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Directed Ports
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6085 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
